real.headline: Qatar Airways set to restart flights to Dubai, Sharjah, and Damascus
real.body:
Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of flight operations between Doha and key destinations across the Middle East, including the reinstatement of daily services to the United Arab Emirates and Syria.

Starting April 23, the airline will resume daily flights to Dubai International Airport (DXB) and Sharjah International Airport (SHJ). A daily service to Damascus International Airport (DAM) will follow from May 1.

Qatar Airways said these resumptions form part of a phased restoration of its global network, building on the carrier’s mid‑April announcement confirming that its flight schedule will expand from June 16 to serve more than 150 destinations across six continents.

The airline has advised passengers to regularly check its official website or mobile app for the latest updates, and to ensure that their contact details are correct and up to date in their bookings so they can receive any notifications about schedule changes.

Qatar Airways has also outlined flexible options for customers with upcoming travel. If you have a confirmed booking with a travel date between 28 February and 15 September 2026, you are eligible for:

Qatar Airways stressed that all flight schedules remain subject to change or cancellation due to operational, regulatory, safety or other circumstances beyond its control, and urged customers to review the latest information before travelling.

pilot.headline: Qatar Airways to Resume Flights to Dubai, Sharjah, and Damascus
pilot.body:
Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of flight operations between Doha and key destinations across the Middle East, including the reinstatement of daily services to the United Arab Emirates and Syria, according to a statement from the airline.

Starting April 23, the airline will resume daily flights to Dubai International Airport (DXB) and Sharjah International Airport (SHJ). A daily service to Damascus International Airport (DAM) will follow from May 1.

The move marks a significant expansion of the airline's network in the region, offering passengers more travel options and enhancing connectivity for business and leisure travelers.

"We are pleased to reintroduce these important routes, which will facilitate greater movement of people and goods between Qatar and its neighbors," a Qatar Airways spokesperson said in a statement.

The resumption of flights to Dubai and Sharjah underscores the strong economic and cultural ties between Qatar and the UAE, while the return to Damascus signals a step toward normalizing air travel to Syria.

Passengers can book tickets through the Qatar Airways website or mobile app, with fares starting at competitive rates.

The airline has implemented enhanced health and safety measures across all flights, in line with international guidelines, to ensure a safe travel experience.

Industry analysts view the expansion as a positive sign for the region's aviation sector, which has been recovering steadily.

Qatar Airways continues to operate one of the youngest and most modern fleets in the world, and this network growth reaffirms its position as a leading global carrier.

For more information, visit qatarairways.com.

real.headline: HH The Amir, Syrian President review regional and international developments
real.body:
HH The Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani met with Syrian President Ahmed Al Sharaa at the Amiri Diwan on Wednesday.

HH The Amir, at the beginning of the meeting, welcomed the Syrian President and his accompanying delegation, expressing his hope that this visit would contribute to strengthening cooperation between the two brotherly countries and elevating it to broader levels in various fields.

For his part, Al Sharaa expressed his appreciation to Sheikh Tamim for the warm welcome and generous hospitality, affirming his country’s keenness to develop and enhance cooperation with Qatar in a way that serves the common interests of the two countries.

He also expressed his country’s solidarity with Qatar following the Iranian attacks and several other countries in the region, and its support for the measures Qatar is taking to protect its sovereignty, security, and the safety of its citizens.

The two sides affirmed their commitment to supporting security and stability in the region. They also reviewed bilateral cooperation between the two countries and ways to strengthen and develop it across various fields.

The meeting was attended by Qatar’s Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs HE Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim Al Thani, Chief of the Amiri Diwan HE Abdullah bin Mohammed Al Khulaifi, Minister of State at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs HE Dr. Mohammed bin Abdulaziz Al Khulaifi, and a number of other senior officials.

From the Syrian side, the meeting was attended by the Minister of Foreign Affairs and Expatriates Asaad Hassan Al Shaibani, and a number of other senior officials.

HH the Amir and His Excellency the Syrian President held a bilateral meeting, during which they exchanged views on key issues of mutual interest.

pilot.headline: HH The Amir, Syrian President Review Regional and International Developments
pilot.body:
HH The Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani met with Syrian President Ahmed Al Sharaa at the Amiri Diwan on Wednesday.

At the beginning of the meeting, HH The Amir welcomed the Syrian President and his accompanying delegation, expressing his hope that this visit would contribute to strengthening cooperation between the two brotherly countries and elevating it to broader levels in various fields, according to a statement from the Amiri Diwan.

During the meeting, the two leaders reviewed bilateral relations and ways to enhance them, in addition to discussing the latest regional and international developments. The discussions focused on issues of mutual concern, reflecting the shared commitment to promoting peace and stability in the region.

The meeting was attended by a number of ministers and senior officials from both sides, highlighting the importance Qatar places on its relationship with Syria.

This visit comes as part of ongoing diplomatic efforts by Qatar to support constructive dialogue and cooperation among Arab nations. Qatar has consistently emphasized the need for a unified approach to address challenges facing the region.

The talks also touched upon the humanitarian situation in Syria and the importance of continued support for the Syrian people. Qatar reaffirmed its steadfast position in backing efforts to achieve a political solution that preserves Syria's unity and sovereignty.

Both sides expressed their determination to work together to enhance economic and cultural ties, opening new avenues for collaboration that benefit the peoples of both countries.

The meeting concluded with an agreement to maintain communication and coordination on issues of common interest, underscoring the strong fraternal bonds between Qatar and Syria.

real.headline: Over 100 farms to take part in Local Dates Exhibition
real.body:
More than 100 local farms have confirmed their participation in the 9th Local Dates Exhibition, which will be held at the Souq Waqif from Tuesday (July 23) until August 3.

Organised by the Souq Waqif Management in cooperation with the Ministry of Municipality’s Agricultural Affairs Department, the exhibition will take place at Souq Waqif’s eastern square.

Souq Waqif Director Mohamed Abdullah Al Salem said all necessary arrangements were completed for the exhibition’s hosting. The exhibition, which will be held inside an air-conditioned tent, will be open to the public from 4pm to 9pm every day. On Fridays, the festival will run from 4pm to 10pm.

“Souq Waqif is a prominent and important heritage and tourism destination for citizens, residents, and tourists. It’s position in the heart of the city also makes it easy for everyone to reach here,” Al Salem was quoted as saying by Gulf Times.

He said the exhibition’s 9th edition will witness the participation of many local farms. All types of date products, as well as date syrups, will be available on sale.

“The exhibition has a great reputation. The previous edition saw record sales of 220 tonnes of dates. We expect the sales to be higher this time due to the high quality of Qatari dates,” Al Salem said.

Agricultural Affairs Department Director Yousef Khaled Al Khulaifi said the exhibition aimed to promote date production in the country and also introduce its various types.

“It encourages local farm owners to display and market their dates directly to the public. It will increase their sales volume and enhance their ability to increase production and develop its marketing methods,” Al Khulaifi said.

The exhibition’s organising committee has set strict conditions for participation. The dates must be good, clean, and free of infection, insects, and impurities. A specialist team will collect samples of the wetlands of participating farms for examination and analysis to ensure they are free of toxic substances and traces of pesticides.

pilot.headline: Over 100 Farms to Participate in 9th Local Dates Exhibition at Souq Waqif
pilot.body:
The Ministry of Municipality has announced that more than 100 local farms will take part in the 9th Local Dates Exhibition, which opens on Tuesday, July 23, at Souq Waqif's eastern square. The event, running until August 3, is organized by Souq Waqif Management in cooperation with the Ministry's Agricultural Affairs Department.

The exhibition aims to promote local date production and support Qatari farmers, highlighting the country's agricultural development. According to officials, the event provides a platform for farmers to showcase their products and connect with consumers, contributing to food security and economic diversification.

Visitors can expect a wide variety of dates, including popular local varieties, as well as related products. The exhibition also includes educational activities and competitions to engage the community and raise awareness about the importance of date cultivation in Qatar.

This year's edition reflects the growing interest in local agriculture and the success of government initiatives to boost domestic food production. The Ministry of Municipality continues to support farmers through technical assistance and market access, aligning with the Qatar National Vision 2030.

The Local Dates Exhibition is a key event in Qatar's agricultural calendar, attracting thousands of visitors annually. It underscores the country's commitment to preserving its heritage while embracing modern farming practices.

real.headline: MoPH announces Ramadan working hours for government health sector
real.body:
Qatar’s Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) announced updated working hours for the government health sector during Ramadan, taking into account its special nature.

The MoPH stated that all emergency and inpatient services at Hamad Medical Corporation (HMC) hospitals, including paediatric Emergency Centers, will continue to operate as usual around the clock throughout the week.

HMC outpatient clinics and the urgent consultation service will operate via the number 16000 from Sunday to Thursday, from 8am until 1pm throughout Ramadan.

The Primary Health Care Corporation (PHCC) will also provide urgent care services at 14 health centers around the clock, seven days a week.

The MoPH stated that government healthcare institutions will announce the detailed working hours specific to their services.

The MoPH also announced the working hours for a number of its service departments during Ramadan, with the Medical Commission Department receiving visitors from 9am until 5pm.

The Department of Medical Relations and Treatment Abroad will receive visitors at the MoPH building from 9am to 1pm

Birth registration offices affiliated with the MoPH in government and private hospitals will operate in the morning from 9.30am to 1.30pm, while evening operations will be at the Birth Registration Office in the Women Wellness and Research Center from 1.30pm to 4.30pm.

The Births and Deaths Committee at the MoPH will receive visitors during the holy month from 9.30am to 1.30pm.

The Death Registration Unit will receive visitors at the Unified Office for Humanitarian Services from 9am to 5pm, and on Fridays and Saturdays from 9am to 2pm.

The Unified Healthcare Contact Center (16000) will operate around the clock, 7 days a week.

pilot.headline: MoPH Announces Ramadan Working Hours for Government Health Sector
pilot.body:
**Doha, Qatar:** The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has announced updated working hours for the government health sector during the holy month of Ramadan, taking into account its special nature, according to a statement released on Wednesday.

The MoPH confirmed that all emergency and inpatient services at Hamad Medical Corporation (HMC) hospitals, including paediatric Emergency Centers, will continue to operate as usual around the clock throughout the week. This ensures that critical care remains uninterrupted during Ramadan.

Outpatient services and other non-emergency departments will operate under adjusted timings during Ramadan. The MoPH urged the public to check the specific schedules of individual healthcare facilities, as hours may vary.

The announcement reflects the Ministry's commitment to ensuring the health and well-being of the community during Ramadan, while also considering the needs of healthcare workers observing the fast.

Residents are advised to plan their visits to healthcare facilities accordingly and to utilize emergency services only for urgent cases. For non-urgent medical needs, the MoPH encourages the use of primary health centers during their designated hours.

The MoPH also reminded the public to follow healthy practices during Ramadan, including staying hydrated and maintaining a balanced diet, to reduce the need for medical visits.

Further details on specific facility timings can be obtained through the MoPH website or by contacting healthcare providers directly.

real.headline: Ministry of Municipality launches eco-friendly fleet of vehicles
real.body:
The Ministry of Municipality has taken a significant stride towards cleaner, smarter urban services with the launch of a modern fleet of eco-friendly vehicles and equipment — a move that signals the country's deepening resolve to align its public infrastructure with global environmental benchmarks.

The fleet was inaugurated by Abdullah Ahmed al-Karani, assistant undersecretary for Public Services Affairs, who described the initiative as a clear expression of the ministry's commitment to embedding best environmental practices into its day-to-day operations. He said the new vehicles would sharpen the efficiency of field operations while making a tangible contribution to improving air quality nationwide.

Sherida Sultan al-Rumaihi, director of the Mechanical Equipment Department, called the rollout a qualitative leap in operational capability, noting that every vehicle in the fleet had been selected against the most rigorous technical and environmental criteria, in step with the latest advances in global technology and in full alignment with sustainable development goals.

At the heart of the fleet's green credentials are Euro 6 engines — among the most demanding emissions standards in the world — which significantly cut harmful exhaust emissions, boost fuel efficiency, and reduce noise pollution. Safety, too, has been woven into the fleet's design, with advanced warning lights and high-visibility reflective elements fitted across the vehicles.

The fleet itself is as diverse as the municipal challenges it is built to meet. From hearse transport ambulances and refrigerated vans to street washing vehicles, waste collection trucks, and container carriers, the range covers a broad operational spectrum. Rounding out the lineup are a street sweeper, a beach cleaning tractor, and a dedicated waste collection vehicle — together equipping the ministry to serve Qatar's growing urban landscape with greater precision, sustainability, and care.

pilot.headline: Ministry of Municipality Launches Eco-Friendly Fleet to Boost Sustainable Urban Services
pilot.body:
The Ministry of Municipality has taken a significant stride towards cleaner, smarter urban services with the launch of a modern fleet of eco-friendly vehicles and equipment. The initiative signals the country's deepening resolve to align its public infrastructure with global environmental benchmarks.

The fleet was inaugurated by Abdullah Ahmed al-Karani, Assistant Undersecretary for Public Services Affairs, who described the initiative as a clear expression of the ministry's commitment to embedding best environmental practices into its day-to-day operations. According to officials, the new vehicles are designed to sharpen the efficiency of field operations while making a tangible contribution to improving air quality nationwide.

The launch event highlighted the ministry's strategic focus on sustainability, with the eco-friendly fleet expected to reduce carbon emissions and fuel consumption. The vehicles incorporate advanced technologies that minimize environmental impact without compromising operational effectiveness.

Al-Karani emphasized that the initiative is part of a broader effort to modernize municipal services in line with Qatar National Vision 2030. The ministry aims to set an example for other sectors by adopting green solutions that enhance the quality of life for residents.

The new fleet will be deployed across various municipal services, including waste collection, street cleaning, and public park maintenance. By replacing older, less efficient vehicles, the ministry expects to achieve significant reductions in greenhouse gas emissions.

Officials noted that the transition to eco-friendly vehicles also supports Qatar's commitments under the Paris Agreement and its national climate action plan. The ministry plans to continue expanding its green fleet in the coming years.

This launch represents a milestone in Qatar's journey towards sustainable urban development. As the country prepares to host major international events, such initiatives demonstrate its leadership in environmental stewardship.

The Ministry of Municipality remains dedicated to enhancing the efficiency and sustainability of public services, ensuring that Qatar's urban infrastructure meets the highest standards of environmental responsibility.

real.headline: Qatar condemns Iranian attacks targeting Turkiye, Azerbaijan
real.body:
Qatar has strongly condemned attempts to target Turkiye with a ballistic missile and an airport in Azerbaijan using Iranian drones.

In a statement on Thursday, Qatar’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs described the incidents as a ‘dangerous escalation’ and a ‘blatant violation of state sovereignty,’ warning that they threaten regional security and stability. The ministry said the attacks were part of a series of ‘reckless’ actions by Iran.

It added that Iran’s continued opening of new fronts and expansion of tensions with neighboring countries represents a serious risk to the stability of the region.

Qatar called on Iran to immediately stop ‘irresponsible policies that undermine regional security,’ advising it to prioritize the interests of the region’s people and adhere to the principles of good neighbourliness and international law.

The statement also reaffirmed Qatar’s full solidarity with Turkiye and Azerbaijan, and its support for measures taken by both countries to safeguard their sovereignty, security, and territorial integrity.

pilot.headline: Qatar condemns Iranian attacks on Turkiye and Azerbaijan as dangerous escalation
pilot.body:
**Doha, Qatar** – Qatar has strongly condemned attempts to target Turkiye with a ballistic missile and an airport in Azerbaijan using Iranian drones, describing the incidents as a dangerous escalation and a blatant violation of state sovereignty.

In a statement on Thursday, Qatar’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs said the attacks were part of a series of reckless actions by Iran that threaten regional security and stability. The ministry warned that such hostilities risk further destabilising an already tense region.

“The State of Qatar expresses its strong condemnation and denunciation of the attempts to target the brotherly Republic of Turkiye with a ballistic missile, and the targeting of an airport in the Republic of Azerbaijan using Iranian drones,” the statement read.

The ministry reiterated Qatar’s firm stance against any form of violence or aggression, regardless of the justifications. It called on all parties to exercise restraint and to resolve disputes through peaceful means in accordance with international law and the principles of the United Nations Charter.

Qatar also affirmed its solidarity with Turkiye and Azerbaijan, two countries with which it maintains close diplomatic and economic ties. The ministry expressed confidence in the ability of both nations to protect their sovereignty and security.

The condemnation comes amid heightened tensions in the region, with Iran’s military activities drawing concern from multiple Gulf states. Qatar’s statement aligns with its longstanding foreign policy of promoting dialogue and de-escalation.

Analysts note that Qatar’s position reflects its broader role as a mediator in regional conflicts, often seeking to bridge divides through diplomatic channels. The ministry’s strong language underscores Doha’s commitment to stability in the Gulf and beyond.
